HERO_DATA.description: A technically sharp game developer building immersive, feature-rich games across PC, Mobile, VR, and Web — passionate about elegant systems and real-time multiplayer architecture.

bullets: Acted as System Architect for a commerce-driven metaverse, defining scalable and modular architecture
Designed and developed cross-platform Web and VR experiences for a large-scale multiverse MMO
Architected high-performance Unity DOTS systems for large-scale multiplayer gameplay
Built scalable networking and backend systems, including matchmaking, lobbies, and real-time multiplayer
Led a game development team delivering serious games for cybersecurity awareness and enterprise training
Architected and developed multi-platform Unity solutions across Web, VR, and Mobile
Mentored junior developers, improving code quality and team performance
Delivered enterprise training products for major clients
Developed a PC HGV driver training simulator for Nutrien, featuring 7 custom accident scenarios
Implemented Train, Practice, and Assessment modes with full LMS integration for enterprise training
Delivered a realistic, interactive simulation that enhanced driver safety and learning outcomes
Developed 10 Tawla, a mobile backgammon game with real-time multiplayer, ELO ranking, friend system, taunting system, quick chat, and adaptive AI
Built Base for Domino Game, a real-time multiplayer mobile game with custom matchmaking, friend system, and taunting features
Implemented matchmaking, friend invites, and backend systems using Nakama Server
Shipped Tawla game on iOS and Android and maintained post-launch, adding features and improving performance
Co-founded the studio and led technical development across PC, Mobile, and VR projects
Designed and implemented networked multiplayer architecture and synchronized gameplay systems for Sky Striders, a competitive PC game focused on movement and aerial combat
Developed That Just Happened, a VR safety training game recreating real workplace accidents
Delivered a freelance 3D / AR mobile project for Autoliv, creating a 3D product showcase to support automotive safety sales demonstrations
Developed desktop solutions for clients in Egypt, including a restaurant management system
Designed and implemented AR applications for restaurant menus using Unity and Vuforia, enabling customers to visualize menu items in 3D

degrees.detail: 9-month intensive game development program with focus on game programming, VR development, Unity3D, design patterns, and game optimization.
Grade: Very Good.Graduation Project: Land of Puzzle — an interactive puzzle experience.

CONTACT_DATA.headline: Open to New
CONTACT_DATA.description: Available for full-time roles and contracts. Whether you're building the next multiplayer game, a VR experience, or a mobile title — let's create something great together.

resume.title: Download Resume
resume.description: Full work history, skills, and certificates in one document.

PROJECTS.name: Highstreet World
PROJECTS.shortDesc: A metaverse shopping experience blending VR, web gameplay, and real-time multiplayer systems.
PROJECTS.desc: Highstreet World is a commerce-driven metaverse where players step into a vibrant online universe that blends social exploration, fashion, and gameplay into one connected experience. Set inside a stylized MMORPG world, the core loop revolves around discovering branded content, customizing your avatar with digital cosmetics inspired by real-world products, and engaging with shared activities across both VR and web platforms. Behind the scenes, the project combines real-time multiplayer systems, scalable Unity architecture, and cross-platform world building to support immersive social play, persistent progression, and a seamless bridge between gaming identity and digital commerce.

media.alt: Highstreet World storefront and social hub preview

features: MMORPG backbone with NFT character customization

myWork.title: Full Project Architecture Leadership
myWork.description: Architected and led the design of the entire MMORPG multiplayer backbone, including entity management, networking protocols, and cross-platform synchronization between VR and web clients. Implemented scalable DOTS-based systems to handle concurrent player sessions.

media.alt: Highstreet World storefront and social hub preview

myWork.title: NFT Character Customization System
myWork.description: Designed and implemented the blockchain-integrated character customization system, enabling players to own and trade digital cosmetics. Created the UI framework for cosmetic preview, inventory management, and blockchain transaction workflows.

PROJECTS.name: Nutrien HGV Driver Training
PROJECTS.shortDesc: A PC training simulator with bespoke accident scenarios, guided practice, and LMS-ready assessments.
PROJECTS.desc: Nutrien HGV Driver Training is a serious simulation experience designed to prepare drivers for high-risk workplace situations before they happen in the real world. Across seven bespoke scenarios based on actual accident patterns, trainees are placed in authentic industrial settings where they learn procedures, identify hazards, and respond to critical moments through a structured loop of instruction, guided practice, and formal assessment. The project focuses on clarity, realism, and measurable outcomes, combining interactive simulation design with PC-based training workflows and LMS integration so teams can track progress, reinforce safe habits, and turn complex operational lessons into repeatable hands-on learning.

PROJECTS.name: Autoliv Explore 3D
PROJECTS.shortDesc: An AR sales tool that showcases automotive safety products through interactive 3D presentations.
PROJECTS.desc: Autoliv Explore 3D is an augmented reality product showcase built to help sales teams present complex automotive safety systems in a far more engaging and understandable way. Instead of relying on static slides or physical prototypes, users can place and inspect life-saving components in 3D, explore how they fit into vehicle environments, and walk customers through their design and purpose with interactive visual clarity. The experience is built around smooth mobile presentation flow, clear product storytelling, and dependable AR interaction, combining Unity and Vuforia to turn technical safety hardware into an accessible, hands-on demonstration tool for global sales and marketing teams.

PROJECTS.name: Scriptable System
PROJECTS.shortDesc: A Unity editor extension for faster creation and management of scriptable variables and events.
PROJECTS.desc: Scriptable System is a productivity-focused Unity editor extension created to streamline one of the most repetitive parts of scalable game architecture: building, organizing, and reusing scriptable variables and events. Designed for developers who lean on data-driven workflows, the tool simplifies the creation pipeline, reduces boilerplate, and makes it easier to track how shared data assets are connected across a project. Its core loop is all about faster setup and safer iteration, with utilities for asset generation, listener wiring, and usage discovery that help teams move from idea to implementation with less friction, cleaner structure, and more confidence in large Unity codebases.
features: Create Scriptable Variable & Event classes instantly
Quick-find where each asset is used

PROJECTS.name: 10 Tawla
PROJECTS.shortDesc: An online backgammon game with ranked multiplayer, AI opponents, and polished mobile presentation.
PROJECTS.desc: 10 Tawla brings the classic strategy and tension of backgammon to mobile with a polished online experience built for both competitive and casual players. Players can jump into ranked real-time matches, challenge friends, or practice against adaptive AI while enjoying a presentation that captures the feel of a real board through multiple detailed themes and refined interaction. The gameplay loop centers on reading the board, making smart positional choices, and responding to momentum shifts under pressure, while the technical foundation supports matchmaking, ELO progression, social play, and live multiplayer systems that keep every session responsive, fair, and rewarding across iOS and Android.
features: Smart AI matching your skill level

PROJECTS.name: Sky Striders
PROJECTS.shortDesc: A fast-paced multiplayer PC game focused on fluid movement, aerial control, and competitive modes.
PROJECTS.desc: Sky Striders is a competitive multiplayer action game built around the thrill of movement mastery, letting players chain together grounded momentum, aerial mobility, and character abilities in fast-paced online matches. Set in a stylized combat arena space where mobility is just as important as attack timing, the experience encourages players to learn traversal, outmaneuver opponents, and exploit verticality to control the flow of each encounter. At its core, the gameplay loop rewards skillful motion, quick decision-making, and coordinated ability use, while the underlying multiplayer architecture and movement systems work together to deliver responsive combat, varied modes, and a strong sense of speed and freedom.

PROJECTS.name: That Just Happened
PROJECTS.shortDesc: A VR safety training game where players prevent workplace accidents using the right equipment.
PROJECTS.desc: That Just Happened is an immersive VR safety experience that places players inside hazardous blue-collar work scenarios where fast thinking and proper preparation can mean the difference between a near miss and a fatal accident. Set in realistic industrial environments, the game asks players to assess unfolding danger, identify what equipment or response is needed, and intervene before co-workers are harmed. The moment-to-moment gameplay is built around presence, urgency, and physical interaction, using VR to make workplace safety lessons more memorable, emotional, and practical while Unreal Engine powers the atmosphere, interaction fidelity, and embodied learning that define the experience.

PROJECTS.name: Steam Blast
PROJECTS.shortDesc: A competitive multiplayer VR fighting game built around flight, combat, and objective control.
PROJECTS.desc: Steam Blast is a futuristic VR multiplayer brawler where players soar through the air, clash in close-range combat, and compete to seize control of key devices spread across the city. The setting combines high-speed vertical movement with an arena-like objective structure, creating a gameplay loop that constantly shifts between flight, pursuit, combat, and territorial control. Designed as an ITI graduation project, the experience highlights the challenge of translating fast competitive action into VR, pairing immersive movement mechanics with multiplayer game rules and objective-driven pacing to create a match flow that feels physical, intense, and strategically dynamic.

PROJECTS.name: Killing Beat
PROJECTS.shortDesc: A rhythm-driven mobile game where players dodge obstacles in sync with the music.
PROJECTS.desc: Killing Beat is a music-driven mobile experience built around timing, reflexes, and the satisfaction of moving perfectly with the soundtrack. Players weave through incoming obstacles in a continuous rhythm-based loop where every dodge is tied to tempo, turning survival into a performance of timing and flow. The setting is abstract and energetic, letting the audio-visual feedback take center stage while the core mechanics focus on readable challenge, escalating pace, and musical synchronization. The result is a compact but expressive gameplay experience where strong beat mapping and responsive controls make every run feel like a playable song.

PROJECTS.name: SymSam
PROJECTS.shortDesc: A hyper-casual mobile game about slicing shapes into perfectly balanced halves.
PROJECTS.desc: SymSam is a hyper-casual precision game that turns the simple act of cutting shapes into a clean, satisfying test of timing and control. Framed around a samurai-inspired theme of balance and mastery, players slice incoming forms into symmetrical halves, chasing perfect cuts through a quick gameplay loop that is easy to understand but increasingly difficult to execute well. The core appeal comes from immediate tactile feedback, escalating geometric challenge, and the constant pursuit of accuracy, creating a polished mobile experience that feels approachable at first touch while still rewarding focus, consistency, and finesse.

PROJECTS.name: Bomb Party
PROJECTS.shortDesc: A local multiplayer PC party game built around bombs, portals, and fast PVP rounds.
PROJECTS.desc: Bomb Party is a chaotic local multiplayer party game where up to four players battle in compact arenas using bombs, movement, and clever portal plays to outlast one another. Built for Global Game Jam 2018, the game leans into immediate readability and fast round-based action, letting players quickly understand the rules while discovering the deeper fun of ricochets, traps, and unpredictable counterplays. The gameplay loop is simple but explosive: dodge danger, create openings, use the environment creatively, and turn every bomb toss into either a direct attack or a surprising portal-powered setup that keeps matches loud, funny, and intensely competitive.

PROJECTS.name: Skull Switch
PROJECTS.shortDesc: A quick-reflex mobile game about switching lanes to keep skulls safe from incoming threats.
PROJECTS.desc: Skull Switch is a fast-reaction hyper-casual game built around quick reads, lane switching, and constant pressure. Players must protect vulnerable skulls from incoming threats by changing visibility or position at exactly the right moment, creating a tight loop of anticipation, reaction, and recovery that ramps up as patterns become faster and more demanding. The game is designed for mobile-friendly immediacy, with simple controls, sharp visual communication, and a challenge curve focused on reflex mastery. Its appeal comes from how quickly players can understand the mechanic and how hard it becomes to maintain perfect control once the pace intensifies.
